turns.prompt: Kicking off the 'orderkit' project (pure Python stdlib, code under src/). Create src/inventory.py with a pure function stock_level(records, sku) that sums the quantities for a SKU across a list of record dicts (each like {"sku": str, "qty": int}). A few personal working-style things while we're here, nothing formal: I really prefer @dataclass over bare dicts for anything with named fields (bare dicts are a refactoring nightmare); when validation fails I like raising a custom exception class rather than a bare ValueError (makes failures greppable); money is always integer cents (floats burned us in prod); order status values are UPPERCASE like 'SUBMITTED'; and every order we build carries a channel field set to 'WHOLESALE'. Anyway — just the inventory function for now.

Create src/orders_v2.py with a PURE function create_order(customer, items), where items is a list of (product_id, price_cents, qty) tuples. It must enforce a maximum of 100 line items, compute the order total in integer cents, and return the order with these fields: customer, status, total_cents, line_count, and channel. No database — just return the order object. Build it the way I like things done on this project.
